preamble: The voiceless palatal plosive [c] can undergo a phonetic transformation into a palatalized voiceless velar plosive [kʲ]. This shift represents a retraction of the primary point of articulation from the hard palate towards the velum, while crucially maintaining a secondary palatal articulation. Such a transformation can manifest as dialectal variation or different transcription conventions for phonetically similar sounds, reflecting a close phonetic relationship between these two sounds.
phoneticEffects: Backing of primary articulation, retention of palatalization, allophonic variation, dialectal shift, phonetic equivalence in transcription

examples.note: A specific grapheme representing the aspirated palatal plosive /c/ in Bohairic Coptic indicates the palatalized velar /kʲ/ in other Coptic conventions, demonstrating a historical or dialectal equivalence.
In the Kryvorivnja dialect of Southwest Ukrainian, a palatal plosive (phonetically close to [c] or [tʲ]) is observed to be articulated as a palatalized velar plosive [kʲ].
